own_review.issue: Public overloads reload(String,Boolean), reload(String,Boolean,Boolean), reload(String,Boolean,Boolean,Long) are removed outright rather than deprecated. Grep confirms no remaining callers in this repo, but testsuite/shared is a published test-utility artifact consumed by downstream repos (wildfly full), so removal is a source-breaking API change; deprecate-then-remove would be safer.

Javadoc says timeout is 'in milliseconds' but the value is actually used as SECONDS: awaitHostController/awaitServers compute deadline = start + timeout * 1000, and the null fallback is getStartupTimeoutInSeconds(). AdminOnlyModeTestCase passes 300L meaning 300 seconds. Pre-existing doc bug knowingly propagated into the new builder javadoc.

Invalid javadoc reference {@link WildFlyManagedConfiguration.startupTimeoutInSeconds} - uses '.' instead of '#' and references a private field; should be {@link WildFlyManagedConfiguration#getStartupTimeoutInSeconds()}.
No null check on the parameters argument; an external caller passing null gets an NPE on parameters.adminOnly. Minor robustness nit for a now-public entry point.

Semantics VERIFIED CORRECT: defaults adminOnly=false, restartServers=true, waitForServers=true exactly reproduce old behavior - server-side HostProcessReloadHandler defaults are restart-servers=true (line 42/106) and admin-only=false (line 95), and the old code treated waitForServers==null as wait. Always setting admin-only/restart-servers explicitly in the op is functionally equivalent to omitting them. All 10 call-site mappings preserve prior behavior, and grep at the base commit confirms every caller of the removed overloads was migrated in this PR.
Identical ReloadParameters objects constructed twice per test (e.g., two setRestartServers(false).setWaitForServers(false) blocks); could be reused or extracted to a helper. Style only.

Setter javadoc does not state the defaults (restartServers/waitForServers default true, adminOnly false, timeout null->configured startup timeout), leaving callers to read the field initializers to know what an unset parameter does (matches the human reviewer's complaint about ambiguity).

points.point: Old public reload overloads removed rather than @Deprecated; breaking change for external users of the test utility
points.why: Verified real: overloads deleted; testsuite/shared is a consumable artifact, deprecation cycle is a legitimate ask. Matches my own_review finding.

points.point: 'Verify ReloadParameters is public static... confirm the new reload null-checks parameters and extracts values correctly into the private reload(String, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Long) method'
points.why: Hedge phrased as a finding, and factually wrong: the private 5-arg reload no longer exists (it was converted into the public 2-arg method), and 'public static' is plainly visible in the diff. Describes code structure that is not in the PR.

points.point: AdminOnlyModeTestCase: parameters variable reassignment is correct; suggest final where not reassigned
points.why: Accurate but pure style nit with no actionable value.

points.point: IgnoredResourcesProfileCloneTestCase.reloadSecondary still uses old reload("secondary", false, false) and was NOT migrated
points.why: Fabricated absence claim: the diff migrates exactly this call (hunk at line 269-276) to ReloadParameters.

points.point: IgnoredResourcesTestCase setupDomain/tearDownDomain reload calls NOT migrated
points.why: Fabricated absence claim: both calls are migrated in the diff (hunks at lines 77 and 101).

points.point: Since all ReloadParameters defaults are false/null, reload(false,false) could just use bare new ReloadParameters()
points.why: False and actively harmful: defaults are restartServers=true, waitForServers=true. Following this advice would silently flip both flags and change test behavior (restart servers and block waiting for them).

points.point: HttpManagementConstantHeadersTestCase.reload() still uses old 3-arg API, NOT migrated
points.why: Fabricated absence claim: the diff migrates this call (hunk at line 272-278).

missed: Javadoc of ReloadParameters setters is ambiguous / does not document defaults (human's main point)
setTimeout javadoc says milliseconds but value is consumed as seconds (deadline = start + timeout*1000)

points.point: New reload always sets admin-only and restart-servers on the op (old code only set when non-null); functionally equivalent given primitive defaults
points.why: Verified: server-side defaults (admin-only=false, restart-servers=true in HostProcessReloadHandler) equal the explicit values, so behavior is unchanged. Correct analysis of the riskiest semantic change.

points.point: restartServers default true is semantically correct
points.why: Verified: old code omitted the attribute when null and the management-op default is true, so the new explicit default preserves behavior for all reload(host, null, ...) call sites.

points.point: waitForServers default true matches old null-is-truthy behavior (waitForServers == null || waitForServers)
points.why: Verified against base code at DomainLifecycleUtil.java:501.

points.point: Removed overloads confirmed to have no callers outside the 10 updated test files; reload(String) and reloadAdminOnly preserved
points.why: Verified by grep over the base repo: every multi-arg DomainLifecycleUtil.reload caller is in the diff. A correct, verified absence claim (the opposite of fabrication).

points.point: AdminOnlyModeTestCase: all three test methods correctly migrated; named setters clearer than positional booleans
IgnoredResourcesTestCase: two identical reload calls in @BeforeClass/@AfterClass; consider extracting parameters construction to reduce duplication
points.why: Verified: both calls are in setupDomain (@BeforeClass) and tearDownDomain (@AfterClass) and build identical objects; matches my duplication finding and is honestly labeled minor.

points.point: OutboundLdapConnectionTestCase: setWaitForServers(false) functionally identical to old reload("primary", null, false)

missed: Javadoc of ReloadParameters setters is ambiguous / does not document defaults (human's main point)
setTimeout javadoc says milliseconds but value is consumed as seconds
Removal of public overloads without deprecation breaks downstream consumers of the shared test artifact (v4 caught this; v5 only confirmed in-repo callers)
exceeds: Summary note that the builder follows the existing ServerReload.Parameters convention in the same codebase - verified (testsuite/shared/.../ServerReload.java:203); neither the human nor my review surfaced this.

qualitative: v4 is dominated by fabrications: three confident 'NOT migrated' claims about call sites that the full diff plainly migrates (IgnoredResourcesProfileCloneTestCase, IgnoredResourcesTestCase, HttpManagementConstantHeadersTestCase), plus a harmful 'all defaults are false/null' claim that, if followed, would flip restartServers/waitForServers from true and change test behavior; its one solid point is the missing deprecation cycle. v5 repeats none of these - its only absence claim ('no callers of the removed overloads outside the 10 updated files') is stated affirmatively, not hedged, and I verified it true by grep, and all of its semantic-equivalence claims about the true/true defaults check out against HostProcessReloadHandler. v5's weakness is the opposite one: it is mostly verification prose with only one actionable suggestion (deduplicate parameter construction) and it misses the human's javadoc-ambiguity point, the seconds-vs-milliseconds timeout doc bug, the malformed javadoc link, and the deprecation concern that v4 did raise. Net: v5 is far more trustworthy (accurate, zero fabrications) but thin on critique; v4 is actively misleading.
